amenazas_validez_y_calidad_evidencia.cita_textual: Firstly, the initial construction of high-fidelity virtual simulation models requires substantial computational resources and interdisciplinary expertise, presenting a high barrier to entry. Secondly, the effective operation of the adaptive learning engine depends on a critical mass of student interaction data, meaning its optimization effect is more pronounced in the later stages of course delivery. Additionally, the system's demand for instructor AI literacy necessitates targeted training, as some instructors initially struggled with data interpretation from the teaching dashboard.

comparador_o_condicion_control.ubicacion: Sección 4.4.2. Comparison with Traditional Teaching Approaches (página 21)

instrumentos_evaluacion.cita_textual: the SUS and NASA-TLX questionnaires were administered to assess perceived usability and the cognitive workload associated with the tasks. Quantitative performance indicators were obtained through practical tests and short conceptual assessments applied at the end of each session.
instrumentos_evaluacion.ubicacion: Secciones 4.4.1. Usability and Performance Testing (página 20) y 4.4.2. Comparison with Traditional Teaching Approaches (página 21)

resultados_cognitivos.valor_extraido: Mejora en comprensión conceptual: grupo tradicional 58%, grupo Digital Twin 85% (diferencia de 27 puntos porcentuales); mejora en detección de errores: grupo tradicional 50%, grupo Digital Twin 82% (diferencia de 32 puntos porcentuales)
resultados_cognitivos.cita_textual: For conceptual understanding, the traditional group achieved 58%, compared with 85% in the Digital Twin group, corresponding to a 27-point improvement. Error detection performance also improved significantly, increasing from 50% in the traditional group to 82% with the Digital Twin environment, representing a 32-point advantage.
resultados_cognitivos.ubicacion: Sección 4.4.2. Comparison with Traditional Teaching Approaches (página 21)

resultados_procedimentales.valor_extraido: Mejora en control operacional: grupo tradicional 62%, grupo Digital Twin 88% (diferencia de 26 puntos porcentuales); tiempo promedio de finalización de tarea: 92 s; reducción del 32% en errores operacionales; tasa de precisión del 93%
resultados_procedimentales.cita_textual: In terms of operational control, the traditional group obtained an average score of 62%, while the Digital Twin group reached 88%, representing a difference of 26 percentage points. Operationally, the average task completion time was 92 s, with a 32% reduction in operational errors and a 93% accuracy rate in the executed actions.
resultados_procedimentales.ubicacion: Secciones 4.4.1. Usability and Performance Testing (página 20) y 4.4.2. Comparison with Traditional Teaching Approaches (página 21)

resultados_afectivos_y_engagement.valor_extraido: SUS: 86/100 (excelente); NASA-TLX: 34/100 (bajo a moderado); Usabilidad: M=4.7, SD=0.4; Interacción: M=4.6, SD=0.5; Inmersión: M=4.4, SD=0.6; Motivación: M=4.3, SD=0.6; Aprendizaje activo: grupo tradicional 55%, grupo Digital Twin 90% (diferencia de 35 puntos porcentuales); Confianza: grupo tradicional 60%, grupo Digital Twin 87% (diferencia de 27 puntos porcentuales)
resultados_afectivos_y_engagement.cita_textual: The SUS score reached an average of 86/100, corresponding to an 'excellent' level of usability. The cognitive workload measured with NASA-TLX was 34/100, reflecting a low-to-moderate level suitable for automation-related activities. Usability achieved the highest score (M = 4.7, SD = 0.4). active learning indicators showed a notable increase, with values rising from 55% in the traditional approach to 90% in the Digital Twin-based environment, reflecting a 35-point difference. confidence in executing industrial tasks increased from 60% to 87%, corresponding to a 27-point improvement.

amenazas_validez_y_calidad_evidencia.valor_extraido: Tamaño muestral pequeño, cohorte única, alcance limitado del estudio de caso, falta de generalización estadística, dependencia de hardware Siemens
amenazas_validez_y_calidad_evidencia.cita_textual: The experimental evaluation was conducted with a single cohort of undergraduate engineering students and focused on two representative industrial processes. Although these processes reflect common automation scenarios, the sample size and case-study scope limit the statistical generalizability of the findings. In addition, the current implementation relies on Siemens-based automation hardware, which may constrain direct replication in environments using alternative industrial platforms.

barreras_tecnicas.valor_extraido: Muchos enfoques se basan en modelos completamente simulados sin acoplamiento en tiempo real con controladores físicos industriales como PLC. Otros enfatizan la visualización inmersiva sin descripciones detalladas de las capas de comunicación o mecanismos de sincronización.
barreras_tecnicas.cita_textual: Many approaches rely on fully simulated models without real-time coupling to physical industrial controllers such as PLCs. Others emphasize immersive visualization without detailed architectural descriptions of communication layers or synchronization mechanisms.

barreras_pedagogicas_y_curriculares.valor_extraido: La validación del sistema y la evaluación centrada en el usuario se presentan con frecuencia sin distinguir claramente el rendimiento técnico de los resultados de capacitación medibles.
barreras_pedagogicas_y_curriculares.cita_textual: system validation and user-centered evaluation are frequently presented without clearly distinguishing technical performance from measurable training outcomes.

escalabilidad_y_transferibilidad_reporta.valor_extraido: El marco prioriza plataformas de control y visualización industriales ampliamente adoptadas para facilitar la replicación en laboratorios académicos con diferentes restricciones de recursos y admite escalamiento incremental hacia procesos más complejos, mayor número de usuarios o funcionalidades de monitoreo extendidas.
escalabilidad_y_transferibilidad_reporta.cita_textual: the proposed framework prioritizes widely adopted industrial control and visualization platforms that are representative of real-world automation practice. This design choice facilitates replication in academic laboratories with different resource constraints and supports incremental scaling toward more complex processes, a higher number of users, or extended monitoring functionalities without requiring fundamental architectural modifications.

metadatos.titulo_completo: Digital Twin of a Material Handling System Based on a Physical Construction-Kit Model for Educational Applications
metadatos.autores: Ladislav Rigó, Jana Fabianová, Lucia Cabaníková and Ján Palinský

tipo_entorno_dt.cita_textual: The virtual model created in Tecnomatix Plant Simulation is connected to the physical system controlled by a Siemens PLC SIMATIC S7-1200 and equipped with industrial sensors and an HMI interface. Real-time bidirectional communication is established via the OPC UA protocol using KEPServerEX, ensuring synchronisation between the physical and virtual systems.

sistema_real_representado.cita_textual: This study presents the design, development and experimental verification of a digital twin of a laboratory material handling system.

fidelidad_visual_y_funcional_reportada.cita_textual: Experiments confirmed the functional synchronisation of both systems.

tipo_gemelo_digital_taxonomia_kritzinger.cita_textual: Real-time bidirectional communication is established via the OPC UA protocol using KEPServerEX, ensuring synchronisation between the physical and virtual systems.

componente_fisico.valor_extraido: Siemens PLC SIMATIC S7-1200, sensores (capacitivo, óptico, de vacío, de contacto), actuadores (electromecánicos), HMI touch panel, conveyors, manipulador con ventosa de vacío, plataforma de almacenamiento, switch de red SCALANCE XB005
componente_fisico.cita_textual: a Siemens PLC SIMATIC S7-1200 (by Siemens, Amberg, Germany), a Siemens SIMATIC HMI touch panel (Amberg, Germany), and electro-mechanical components. These electro-mechanical parts include, for example, a capacitive colour sensor, a capacitive sensor at the end of the input conveyor, a capacitive sensor at the end of the output conveyor, an optical colour sensor, contact limit sensors for the end positions of the vertical and horizontal movement of the suction cup, and a vacuum sensor.

componente_virtual.cita_textual: The virtual model created in Tecnomatix Plant Simulation is connected to the physical system... the simulation model developed in Tecnomatix Plant Simulation environment is further supplemented by an external three-dimensional model developed in Autodesk Inventor (version 2026).

grado_sincronizacion_bidireccionalidad.cita_textual: Real-time bidirectional communication is established via the OPC UA protocol using KEPServerEX, ensuring synchronisation between the physical and virtual systems.

arquitectura_capas_modulos.valor_extraido: Capas: física (PLC, sensores, actuadores, HMI), digital (KEPServerEX, OPC UA), virtual (Tecnomatix Plant Simulation, Autodesk Inventor). Módulos: input conveyor, output conveyor, gantry loader, storage platform. Flujo de datos: físico → KEPServerEX → virtual (bidireccional).
arquitectura_capas_modulos.cita_textual: Figure 2 presents a scheme of the essential components, arranged according to functional phases (input, processing, output) and domain levels (cyber, digital, and physical), within the Industry 4.0 concept.

escalabilidad_y_transferibilidad_reporta.valor_extraido: The proposed architecture is based on OPC UA communication, which enables the integration of multiple devices and systems within a unified framework. The use of KEPServerEX further supports extensibility, allowing additional devices—such as robotic systems (e.g., WLKATA Mirobot)—to be incorporated into the digital twin environment without fundamental architectural changes. However, increasing system scale also introduces higher system complexity, particularly in terms of tag management, communication configuration, and data consistency.
